mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering recession fears that suppress equity valuations and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital investment demand, reinforcing economic momentum and generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series in a multivariate regression, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects manufacturing contraction and capacity utilization exhaustion, forcing equity markets to price in imminent economic slowdown.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO declines in late-cycle regimes, or the hypothesis shows negative predictive accuracy (Rank IC < -0.05) specifically during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls demonstrate expanding employment and wage growth, which increases aggregate demand and corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical correlation with 20-day forward returns (Spearman rho < 0.05) across multiple rolling windows, or positive payroll surprises consistently precede negative market moves.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation measured by CPIAUCSL breaching 258.0 triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally by more than 1.5% on average within 20 days following CPIAUCSL crossing 258.0, or the threshold signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with a declining federal funds rate creates a favorable macro environment where economic expansion is supported by cheap credit, amplifying corporate earnings and driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between GDPC1 and FEDFUNDS is statistically insignificant in predicting 60-day returns, or the combined signal underperforms a simple average of the two individual series.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and stagnant payroll growth during a post-shock recovery indicates structural labor market weakness, prompting investors to discount future earnings and sell equities.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns in post-shock recovery periods, or forward returns are positive in >70% of triggered instances, invalidating the structural weakness premise.
